summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Learning & Development professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve people development—through human-centered, systematic approaches.

Facilitate {prog|training} webinars for 600+ participants, boosting program understanding and achieving a 78% program enrollment rate

Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in leave costs, leading to policy redesigns and first aid training
Initiated a DEI pilot with an Inclusive Leadership {prog|workshop} for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to other global locations
Optimized onboarding processes across global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities

jd.meta: University of Oregon — Learning and Development Training Specialist

changeLog: 2026-05-30: Tailored for University of Oregon — Learning and Development Training Specialist (Information Services). Cap-exempt H-1B sponsor; in-state Oregon. Location → Eugene, OR (Open to Relocation). Summary → Learning & Development umbrella. InGenius FIRST + reordered to surface training-material development across media formats first (JD prefers 'training materials in a wide array of media formats — presentations, screencasts, written procedures, accessibility'), live-session+video program reaching 500+, needs assessments, 78% enrollment. NextGen reordered to lead with internal-training consultations. Skills → Learning Systems line foregrounds LMS + screencasts/video + accessibility-aware content design to mirror JD's media-format + LMS + accessibility prefs + basic project management. STRETCH NOTE: this posting is IT-service-adoption training within Information Services; Jamie's L&D is people/program-focused, not IT-systems training. Tailored honestly to transferable training-development, LMS, change-management, and project-management strengths — did not claim IT/technology-training experience she lacks. Cover letter is candid about the people-development angle she brings.
